zh.lifeSummary: Otto van Arkel是Jan V van Arkel的兒子，於1410年出生在荷蘭霍蘭格利姆。他於1434年與Elizabeth Molenaar在荷蘭-拉藍德米德爾可普結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1475年6月6日在荷蘭武特雷特享年65歲。

zh-hans.Middelkoop, Holland, Nederland: 荷兰-拉藍德米德爾可普
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Otto van Arkel是Jan V van Arkel的儿子，于1410年出生在荷兰霍蘭格利姆。他于1434年与Elizabeth Molenaar在荷兰-拉藍德米德爾可普结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1475年6月6日在荷兰武特雷特享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Arkel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Breton: from the Old Breton personal name Arthmael, composed of the elements arth ‘bear’ (in a transferred sense ‘soldier’) + mael ‘prince’, Latinized as Armagilus. This was the name of a Christian saint (5th century) who was the subject of a local cult in Brittany.
Jewish (from Ukraine): artificial name from Yiddish dialect (h)arml ‘hermine’.

variants.name: Ermel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Jurgen, Markus, Matthias.

German: from Middle High German ermel ‘sleeve’, a metonymic occupational name for a sleeve maker. Alternatively, it may have been a nickname for someone who wore clothing with conspicuous sleeves; in medieval times the size of sleeves was regulated by luxury laws.
German: from a pet form of any of the ancient Germanic personal names formed with Erm, from erm, irm ‘all-encompassing, great’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace). Compare Armel 1.

nameMeanings.name: Otto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a short form of any of the various Germanic compound personal names containing the element od, ot ‘prosperity, wealth’ ( compare the corresponding Old English ēad in names such as Edward and Edwin ). St Otto of Bamberg ( d. 1139 ) was a missionary to the Pomeranians. Otto the Great ( 912–73 ) is generally regarded as the founder of the Holy Roman Empire, and the name has been borne by several members of German and Austrian royal houses. It was recorded occasionally among immigrants to England from the Low Countries in the 14th century, but failed to establish itself. Since the 19th century it has again been used occasionally in the English-speaking world, mostly among immigrants from Germanic countries.

historicEvents.description: The courtyard Episcopal palace at Atrecht had witch burnings.

en.lifeSummary: Otto van Arkel was born in 1410, in Gorinchem, Holland, Netherlands as the son of Jan V van Arkel. He married Elizabeth Molenaar in 1434, in Middelkoop, South Holland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 6 June 1475, in Utrecht, Netherlands, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Otto van Arkel est né en 1410, à Gorinchem, Holland, Pays-Bas, fils de Jan V van Arkel. Il a épousé Elizabeth Molenaar en 1434, à Middelkoop, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 6 juin 1475, à Utrecht, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Otto van Arkel wurde 1410 in Gorinchem, Holland, Niederlande, als Sohn von Jan V van Arkel geboren. Er hat 1434 in Middelkoop, Südholland, Niederlande Elizabeth Molenaar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 6. Juni 1475 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Utrecht, Niederlande.

it.lifeSummary: Otto van Arkel è nato il nel 1410 nel Gorinchem, Holland, Paesi Bassi come figlio di Jan V van Arkel. Lui sposato Elizabeth Molenaar nel 1434, nel Middelkoop, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 6 giugno 1475, nel Utrecht, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.Middelkoop, , South Holland, Netherlands: オランダズイド-hオランドミッドエルコオップ

ja.lifeSummary: Otto van Arkelは1410年にオランダホーランドゴリンケムでJan V van Arkelの息子として生まれた。1434年にオランダズイド-hオランドミッドエルコオップでElizabeth Molenaarと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1475年6月6日にオランダウトレチトで65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Otto van Arkel(은)는 1410년 네덜란드 홀랜드 고린체에서 Jan V van Arkel의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1434년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 미델코프에서 Elizabeth Molenaar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1475년 6월 6일 네덜란드 우트레ch트에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Otto van Arkel nasceu em 1410, em Gorinchem, Holland, Países Baixos filho de Jan V van Arkel. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Molenaar em 1434, em Middelkoop, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 6 de junho de 1475, em Utrecht, Países Baixos, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Otto van Arkel родился в 1410 г. в местности горинчем, холланд, Нидерланды. Его отца звали Jan V van Arkel. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Molenaar в 1434 г. в местности мидделкуп, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 6 июня 1475 г. в местности утречт, Нидерланды в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Otto van Arkel nació en 1410, en Gorinchem, Holland, Países Bajos como hijo de Jan V van Arkel. Se casó con Elizabeth Molenaar en 1434, en Middelkoop, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 6 de junio de 1475, en Utrecht, Países Bajos, a la edad de 65 años.
